patter: WLNK twelve-ninety, the Voice of the Shoreline. Fifty-eight and fair, wind out of the southwest, summer folks loading their cars slow as they can make it.
Tides at the drawbridge: high one-fourteen, low seven thirty-eight. Almanac calls slack water for four fifty-two - them that fish it know to be off it by then.
Lost and found: Edith Prewitt's marmalade tom, Dewey, slipped his collar out by the Book Ark. Answers to Dewey, to a can opener, or to nothing, being a cat. Call the shop.
Public Works pulls the swim lines today, so mind the crews down at Rock Neck. Wave to Wren for me.
Anchor Light's got end-of-season pie till it's gone, and Roz says gone means gone.
A little quieter every morning, this time of year. Stay warm, Lantern Neck. You're not alone out here yet.
WLNK twelve-ninety. Fifty-five and gray, fog to burn off by nine, they tell me. They've been wrong before.
Tides: high two-oh-two, low eight twenty-six. Slack around five-forty - mind it if you're on the water.
Still no word on Dewey, Edith's marmalade tom, last seen by the Book Ark. He'll turn up. They always turn up.
Pool drains today over at Rock Neck. Keep the little ones clear of the deep end, empty or no.
Traffic report: there isn't any. First time all year I can say that and mean it kindly.
WLNK, Lantern Neck. Fifty-two and close. Gulls are restless today. Can't say I blame them.
High tide two fifty-three, low nine-fifteen. Slack water at six twenty-eight; the Club'll be down the marsh for it, same as every dusk.
Dewey the marmalade tom is still out. Edith says leave your porch light on - he knows the way home.
Last charter clears the breakwater this afternoon. If you're getting out by water, this is the getting.
WLNK twelve-ninety. Forty-nine and falling.
High three forty-seven. Low ten-oh-eight. Slack, seven nineteen.
Dewey still out. Leave a light.
Docks come up today. That's the news. That's all the news.
WLNK. Forty-seven. Fog by four.
Dewey. Marmalade. Still out.
Boardwalk lights go down tonight. One section. You'll hear it.
WLNK.
Twelve-ninety, Lantern Neck.
...Dewey.
You're listening to...
you're listening.
